lines: QUESTION ID 25762 : series 3 GreenLight Exam
As it relates to options trading, which of the following are correct ?
l. All long and short puts with the same strike price will be out of the money at the same time
II. All long calls and all long puts with the same strike price will be in the money at the same time
Ill. All long puts and short calls with the same strike price will be out of the money at the same time
'V. All long and short calls with the same strike price will be in the money at the same time
A. II and Ill
B. I and II
C. Ill and IV
D. I and IV
CORRECT ANSWER IS: D
YOUR ANSWER IS: C

It is important to note that the terms in the money, at the money and out of the money are not impacted by if the contract is held long or short. All long and short puts with the same strike. will be out of the money at the same time. All
calls and puts with the same strike will be at the money at the same time.

lines: QUESTION ID 25749 : series 3 GreenLight Exam on
An investor who trades single stock futures contracts by establishing both the long and short positions, will be subject to which of the following based on an adverse price move?
A. A long contract marked to the market will be subject to a call that is less than the loss, while a short position marked to the market will be subject to a call that is greater than the loss
B. A long contract marked to the market will be subject to a call that is equal to the loss, while a short position marked to the market will be subject to a call that is greater than the loss
C. Both long and short contracts will be subject to a call that is equal to the loss or to the FCM's margin requirement, whichever is greater
D. Both long and short contracts will be subject to a call that is equal to 120% of the loss or to the FCM's margin requirement, whichever is greater
CORRECT ANSWER IS: A
YOUR ANSWER IS: C

Single stock futures contracts are subject to a margin requirement of 20 percent of the contract value. A long position that falls will require the investor to establish margin equal to 20 percent of the lower market value. While a short
position subject to a call will require the investor to deposit margin to meet the 20 percent requirement based on the new higher market value.

QUESTION ID 25715 : series 3 GreenLight Exam on.
As it relates to processors and spreaders which of the following are considered correct ?
l. The processor sells the output
II. The processor is subject to spread margin
Ill. The spreader sells the input
'V. The spreader is speculating against the processor
A. I and II
B. Ill and IV
C. l, Ill and IV
D. l, II, Ill,
CORRECT ANSWER IS: C
YOUR ANSWER IS: D

All of the choices are correct except a processor is considered to be a bona fide hedger and would be subject to hedge margin requirements not spread margin requirements. When a processor establishes a spread as a hedge by
going long the input and short the output, the trader who sells the spread to the processor is effectively speculating against the processors ability to operate profitably.

lines: QUESTION ID 25698 : series 3 Greenlight Exam
In response to the CPI rising, the FRB has begun increasing interest rates. Which of the following will likely occur as a result of this action ?
l. The U.S dollar will become stronger
II. The price of oil will rise
Ill. The price of silver will fall
'V. U.S exports will fall
A. II and Ill
B. I and IV
C. l, Ill and IV
D. l, II, and IV
CORRECT ANSWER IS: C
YOUR ANSWER IS: B

As the FRB increases interest rates, it increases the value of the dollar relative to other currencies and relative to items priced in dollars as a whole. If the value of the dollar increases it will make US exports less attractive and exports
will fall. As the value of the dollar increases commodities priced in dollars, such as gold, oil and silver will also fall.

lines: QUESTION ID 25747 : series 3 GreenLight Exam ron
As it relates to the carrying charges for a stock index futures contract, which of the following is not considered ?
l. Safekeeping of securities
II. Interest
Ill. Dividends received
'V. Insurance
A. I and IV
B. II and Ill
C. II, and IV
D. l, Ill and IV
CORRECT ANSWER IS: A
YOUR ANSWER IS: D

Stock index futures do not settle with the delivery of any physical certificates. Therefore, there is no need to safekeeping or insurance for any securities. Interest rates determine the cost of financing the purchase of the underlying

securities and therefore they are a carrying charge. Dividends paid on the underlying securities reduce the carrying charge on the long side and increase carrying charges for short sellers of securities. All of these factors are used to
determine fair value for stock index futures.

A trader is seeking to establish a position in the market for a specific commodity. The trader would like to enter orders to both establish and offset the contracts. As it relates to MIT, market if touched orders, which of the following are
true ?
l. An MIT order to buy will be executed if the contract is offered at the MIT price, with no trades taking place at the MIT price
II. An MIT order to buy will be executed if the contract trades at the MIT price
Ill. An MIT order to sell will be executed if the contract is bid at the MIT price, with no trades taking place at the MIT price
'V. An MIT order to sell will be executed if the contract trades at the MIT price
A. II and IV
B. I and Ill
C. I and II
D. l, II, Ill,
CORRECT ANSWER IS: D

MIT, market if touched orders will be executed if the quote is equal to the MIT price or if the contract trades at the MIT price. An MIT order to buy will be executed if the offer equals the MIT price. An MIT order to sell will be executed if the
bid price equals the MIT price.

lines: QUESTION ID 25689 : series 3 Greenlight Exam

An investor who established an inter-market spread in gold futures by going long a gold contract on one exchange and simultaneously going short gold on another exchange, may offset the contracts in an ex pit transaction so long
as the contract months are the same.
( True/False )
CORRECT ANSWER IS: B
YOUR ANSWER IS: A

This is false. The only way to offset the contracts is to execute an order for the contracts on the exchange where the contract position was opened. While the contracts cover the same commodity, the contracts are traded on different
exchanges.

lines: QUESTION ID 25767 : series 3 GreenLightExam ron
As it relates to the NFA which of the follow are correct ?
l. The NFA regulates off floor activities
II. The NFA regulates the handling of orders executed on an exchange
Ill. The president of the NFA is the only person empowered to initiate a member responsibility action and is appointed by Congress
'V. The NFA will handle disputes over financial losses through the reparations process
A. I only
B. I and II
C. II and IV
D. Ill only
CORRECT ANSWER IS: A
YOUR ANSWER IS: C

The only choice that is correct is that the NFA handles off floor activities. The exchanges handle all issues on the trading floor. The president of the NFA is the only person who may initiate a member responsibility action, however the
president of the NFA is not appointed by congress. The CFTC, not the NFA, handles monetary disputes through the reparations process. The NFA handles monetary disputes through the arbitration process.

QUESTION ID 25721 : series 3 GreenLight Exam
As it relates to a long hedger who is seeking to offset the price risk associated with a change in the commodity price, which of the following is correct?
A. The established hedge will reduce the effective sales price, if the basis weakens
B. The established hedge will reduce the effective purchase price, if the basis weakens
C. The established hedge will reduce the effective sales price, if the basis strengthens
D. The established hedge will reduce the effective purchase price, if the basis strengthens
CORRECT ANSWER IS: B
YOUR ANSWER IS: A

When the basis weakens it becomes more negative. A long hedger who has bought a futures contract to hedge the later purchase of the commodity will realize a lower effective purchase price if the basis weakens. The price of the
cash commodity will have fallen by more than the price of the futures contract. This allows the hedger to pay a lower effective purchase price.

lines: QUESTION ID 25759 : series 3 GreenLight Exam on

A dairy purchases 10 March Class Ill milk futures contracts at 16.65 per cwt. Each contract covers 200,000 lbs per contract. If the dairy opts to offset the contracts when March Class Ill milk is at 17.02, the dairy would realize a:
Each contract has a multiplier of $20
A. $14,000 profit
B. $140,000 profit
C. $7,400 profit
D. $74,000 profit
CORRECT ANSWER IS: C
YOUR ANSWER IS: B
The contract was purchased at 16.65 and sold for 17.02 for a profit of .37. Each tick is worth $20. So the profit per contract is 37 X $20 =$740 X 10 contracts

QUESTION ID 25755 : series 3 GreenLight Exam on
A multinational corporations looking to hedge the purchase of Japanese Yen, would realize a benefit from the hedge in all of the following scenarios, except:
A. If the basis weakens
B. If the price of the Yen increases less than the price of the future
C. If the Yen falls in value the loss will be offset by the fall in the futures
D. If the spread between the spot price and the price of the future weakens
CORRECT ANSWER IS: C
YOUR ANSWER IS: B

The purpose of the long hedge is to hedge against a rise in the Yen. If the Yen falls, that is good for the entity that needs to acquire the Yen. However, if the Yen falls and the futures contract falls this would not off set a loss. If the Yen
falls more than the futures contract that would benefit the hedger.

A speculator who has no interest in the underlying commodity would be subject to the most risk, if:
A. the speculator was long a contract subject to a standard delivery notice past the first notice day

CORRECT ANSWER IS: C

speculator who is long a contract with a stopped delivery notice past the first notice day is subject to the most risk.

lines: QUESTION ID 25754 : series 3 GreenLight Exam on

A U.S watch maker needs to purchase components for its watches from a Swiss manufacturer. The order is to be paid for in I million Swiss Francs, 9 months from now. The spot rate is 1.03 and the 9 month futures are 1.07. If the U.S

watch maker established a hedge based on these current prices, and offsets the hedge when the spot rate for Swiss Francs is 1.05 and the futures are 1.06, what was the net result of the hedge? Each contract covers 10,000 Swiss
Francs.
A. The effect purchase price was
B. The effective purchase price was $1,060,000
C. The effective purchase price was $1,070,000
D. The effective purchase price was $1,050,000
CORRECT ANSWER IS: B
YOUR ANSWER IS: A

In this case the hedge did not work and the cost ended up increasing. The company purchased the francs in the spot market at 1.05 and offset the futures hedge at 1.07. The hedge actually went against the company and fell from 1.07
to 1.06. The hedge increased the cost from 1.05 to 1.06. The total cost for 1,000,000 is therefore, $1,060,000.

lines: QUESTION ID 25745 : series 3 GreenLight Exam on
Which of the following parties would not be a short hedger?
A. A long only portfolio manager concerned about current market conditions
B. An investor in Eurodollars worried about a change in credit quality
C. A corporate borrower seeking to raise capital to build a new plant
D. A fund manager long bonds maturing in 3 months who is looking to roll the proceeds into new bonds
CORRECT ANSWER IS: D
YOUR ANSWER IS: C

If a portfolio manager is looking to purchase bonds in the future, the manage would be a long hedger. In this case the manager will be receiving the principal payment in 3 months from the maturing bonds. At that point the manger
wants to buy bonds, so the manger would go long bond futures to protect from a fall in interest rates.

CORRECT ANSWER IS: C

lines: QUESTION ID 25746 : series 3 GreenLight Exam
Assume that all of the parties in the choices listed below need to establish an anticipatory hedge. Upon review, which is not a long hedger?
A. A lender seeking to make a loan to a corporation in the future
B. An investor seeking to invest money in NASDAQ stocks in 3 months
C. A pension manager seeking to buy bonds
D. A refiner who is creating gasoline
CORRECT ANSWER IS: D
YOUR ANSWER IS: B

Of the choice listed, the refiner who is producing gasoline would be a short hedger. Producers of refined products must sell futures to hedge against a fall in the price of the refined product, in this case gasoline. A lender looking to
make a loan in the future is effectively going to buy bonds and would be a long hedger.

lines: QUESTION ID 25724 : series 3 GreenLight Exam Ovronö
Inflation has been rising over the last year, as interest rates increase in the face of inflation, which of the following choices would be false?
A. Money market funds become more attractive
B. Holding inventory becomes more attractive as prices for finished products rise
C. Raw materials and commodity prices increase
D. Imports become more attractive
CORRECT ANSWER IS: B
YOUR ANSWER IS: D

As interest rates increase the the dollar will become stronger making imports more attractive. Higher interest rates will also make money market funds more attractive as they offer higher interest rates. Holding inventory will not be
more attractive as the interest costs to hold the inventory will become more expensive as interest rates increase.
